stories.headline: Mistral releases Voxtral: open-source 4B TTS model with 90ms latency and 9-language voice cloning
stories.summary: Mistral launched Voxtral, a lightweight 4B-parameter text-to-speech model supporting 9 languages with 90ms latency and voice cloning from 3-second samples. The model is available on Hugging Face under open weights and via API at $0.016 per 1k characters, targeting voice agents, customer support, and multilingual applications.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to silent film era orchestras
stories.lateral_explanation: Early silent film theaters hired live orchestras to fill the void left by absent synchronized sound — what seemed like a complete medium (visual storytelling) was actually missing the layer that made it emotionally real. Mistral's Voxtral does the same for AI systems: text generation and visual AI were the silent-film era of agents, and open-weight voice synthesis at near-human latency is the synchronization event that makes conversational AI feel whole. Both transitions happened when the missing modality became cheap, open, and fast enough to be infrastructure.

stories.headline: Google releases Gemini 3 Deep Think API: 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 and gold-level Physics Olympiad performance
stories.summary: Google released Gemini 3 Deep Think via API to researchers and enterprises, achieving 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 and gold-level performance on 2025 Physics and Chemistry Olympiad problems. The model demonstrated real-world utility by identifying logical flaws in peer-reviewed mathematics papers, marking the first time a frontier model has moved from benchmark mastery to active correction of published academic work.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to chess grandmaster blindfold play
stories.lateral_explanation: Blindfold chess grandmasters maintain a complete, dynamic model of a board they cannot see, updating it from verbal move descriptions alone — a feat demonstrating working memory operating at the edge of human cognition without external scaffolding. Gemini 3 Deep Think is doing something structurally parallel: building a complete reasoning model of a complex problem by holding dozens of interdependencies in active memory simultaneously, without visual or diagrammatic aids. Both are tests of whether deep structural understanding can fully substitute for direct observation of the problem space.

stories.headline: Google launches Lyria 3 and Lyria 3 Pro: high-fidelity music generation integrated into Gemini ecosystem
stories.summary: Google released Lyria 3 and Lyria 3 Pro — specialized music generation models producing high-fidelity audio across genres and instrumentation — now available to Gemini Ultra subscribers alongside Deep Think. The dual launch positions Gemini as a multi-modal creative platform, not just a reasoning engine, with music generation becoming a natively available capability rather than a standalone product.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to player piano roll manufacturing
stories.lateral_explanation: In the 1910s, player piano roll manufacturing industrialized musical performance — encoding a virtuoso's playing as physical holes in paper that could be reproduced indefinitely at zero marginal cost. Google Lyria 3 is the digital version of this moment: compressing musical creativity into a generative model that can reproduce style, mood, and structure on demand. The player piano raised the question of what the human's role is when the output is indistinguishable; Lyria raises exactly the same question with the full weight of a frontier model company behind it.

stories.headline: Luma AI Uni-1 outperforms GPT Image 1.5 on RISEBench at 10–30% lower cost
stories.summary: Luma AI's Uni-1 scored 0.51 on RISEBench — outperforming Google's Nano Banana 2 (0.50) and OpenAI's GPT Image 1.5 (0.46) on reasoning-based image benchmarks — while operating at 10–30% lower inference cost than the leading alternatives. The result is the clearest signal yet that frontier-tier image reasoning no longer requires frontier-tier pricing.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to generic drug economics
stories.lateral_explanation: When a pharmaceutical patent expires, generic manufacturers produce the same compound at dramatically lower cost by eliminating discovery expenses — the drug is identical but the economics reshape the entire industry distribution map. Luma AI Uni-1 is the first clear frontier model 'generic' in image reasoning: matching or exceeding the branded model's performance by optimizing the inference stack rather than racing the capability frontier. If this pattern holds, AI industry pricing will look less like a capability race and more like a pharmaceutical market with branded and generic tiers.

stories.headline: OpenAI discontinues Sora mobile app despite Sora 2 model quality — consumer engagement unsustainable
stories.summary: OpenAI is shutting down its Sora mobile app despite Sora 2 being technically impressive for video and audio generation, citing sustained engagement challenges and inability to build a retention loop around raw video generation. The decision is a direct acknowledgment that model capability and consumer product-market fit are distinct problems — and that current video generation, however impressive, hasn't found its everyday use case.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Betamax vs VHS format war
stories.lateral_explanation: Sony's Betamax was technically superior to VHS by most engineering measures but lost the format war because distribution and pricing — not quality — determined consumer adoption at scale. OpenAI is discovering the same principle: Sora 2's capabilities are impressive by benchmark, but a mobile app requires retention mechanics, discovery loops, and use-case clarity that a raw model doesn't automatically provide. The lesson from Betamax was never about tape quality; the lesson from Sora mobile is that model quality alone cannot substitute for product design.

stories.headline: Sakana AI Scientist v2: first fully AI-generated machine learning paper passes rigorous human peer review in Nature
stories.summary: Sakana AI's AI Scientist v2 — which autonomously executes the full ML research lifecycle from hypothesis generation to experiment to written paper — produced the first fully AI-generated scientific paper to pass rigorous human peer review, now published in Nature. The result marks a qualitative threshold: an AI system can now generate knowledge artifacts that the scientific community validates without knowing their origin.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to self-replicating automata theory
stories.lateral_explanation: John von Neumann's 1940s theory of self-replicating automata described machines capable of constructing copies of themselves — not just performing tasks, but reproducing the process that generates tasks. AI Scientist v2 is the research equivalent: a system that executes the full scientific loop including the publication of peer-reviewed work, meaning it can now generate the type of artifacts that train future AI systems. The automaton has crossed the threshold von Neumann described: it can reproduce its own intellectual process.

stories.headline: Shopify Agentic Storefronts launch in ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Microsoft Copilot, and Gemini (March 27)
stories.summary: Starting March 27, Shopify merchants can sell directly inside ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Microsoft Copilot, and the Gemini app through Agentic Storefronts — with real-time pricing, checkout, and inventory syncing embedded in the conversational interface. The launch is the first large-scale deployment of commerce agents inside frontier AI assistants, making purchase completion possible without leaving the conversation.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval guild market booth placement
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval guilds negotiated physical positioning in market squares as the primary determinant of trade volume — proximity to foot traffic was worth more than the quality of most goods. Shopify's Agentic Storefronts are making the same negotiation in AI space: merchants who gain placement inside ChatGPT's conversational flow get the equivalent of the prime booth at the market square, where buyers are already in a decision state. The variable that determines commerce success has shifted — again — from product quality to distribution architecture.

stories.headline: Grok 4.20 tops benchmarks for current-events accuracy with real-time X/web integration
stories.summary: X's Grok 4.20 with deep real-time web integration and improved source attribution scored highest among March 2026 releases on benchmarks measuring accuracy on news published within the past 30 days. The result underscores that Grok's competitive advantage is not reasoning architecture but sourcing architecture — real-time access to X's information firehose gives it a structural edge on current-events tasks that no static training cutoff can match.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Reuters stringers network design
stories.lateral_explanation: Reuters built its 19th-century news dominance not by having better editors but by having more stringers — local correspondents who updated the wire with live information before competitors could access it. Grok 4.20's architecture is structurally the same: X/Twitter gives it real-time information access that no other frontier model has, not because its reasoning is more capable but because its sourcing is faster and wider. The competitive advantage isn't intelligence; it's the wire network.

stories.headline: IQVIA.ai deploys 150 AI agents across 19 of the top 20 pharmaceutical companies
stories.summary: IQVIA launched IQVIA.ai — powered by NVIDIA — with 150+ intelligent agents already deployed across internal teams and client environments at 19 of the top 20 global pharmaceutical companies, handling clinical trial coordination, commercial analytics, and regulatory workflows. The deployment scale makes IQVIA.ai the largest documented AI-agent rollout in the life sciences sector, with agents operating as functional team members rather than tools.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to 1920s scientific management movement
stories.lateral_explanation: Frederick Taylor's scientific management movement broke manufacturing tasks into measurable, repeatable units — optimizing each step independently to produce aggregate efficiency gains that no individual worker could achieve alone. IQVIA's 150 agents deployed across pharma workflows are doing the same to knowledge work: disaggregating clinical and commercial processes into discrete, measurable agent tasks, each optimized independently. The Taylorism of the knowledge economy has arrived, and it runs on autonomous software.

stories.headline: Oracle AI Database 26ai launches Private Agent Factory: no-code enterprise agents on governed data
stories.summary: Oracle released AI Database 26ai with persistent memory for agents and a no-code Private Agent Factory, enabling any enterprise team to deploy autonomous agents for structured data analysis, deep research, and database-aware knowledge tasks without engineering involvement. The Private Agent Factory is the first major enterprise database product to treat agent deployment as a data governance feature rather than a developer feature.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to 18th-century patent office democratization
stories.lateral_explanation: The original patent office was designed so any inventor with a valid claim could receive standardized protection — democratizing access to legal infrastructure that only large enterprises could previously negotiate. Oracle's no-code Private Agent Factory follows the same logic: any enterprise team with data in Oracle's ecosystem can now deploy autonomous agents without a technical team, lowering the barrier from 'only large engineering orgs' to 'any business unit with a data need.' Infrastructure democratization has always preceded capability democratization.

stories.headline: domo.health launches voice-first AI agent for homecare professionals in Zurich — reducing admin to free care time
stories.summary: domo.health unveiled a voice-first AI agent for homecare professionals at a Google Cloud event in Zurich, designed with a human-in-the-loop architecture to handle documentation, scheduling, and administrative workflows through natural voice conversation. The agent's primary design goal is to return clinical time to nurses by removing the administrative distance between care activity and record-keeping.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Nightingale ward redesign
stories.lateral_explanation: Florence Nightingale's hospital ward redesign in the 1850s was fundamentally about reducing the physical distance between nurse and patient — eliminating time nurses spent walking so more time could be spent on care. The domo.health voice agent achieves the same outcome through a different dimension: removing the interface distance between care activity and documentation so nurses can stay present at the bedside rather than retreating to a workstation. Both interventions use environmental or interface design to recover care time that administrative friction was systematically consuming.

stories.headline: Google Cloud at HIMSS 2026: Humana, CVS Health, and Waystar deploying Gemini agents at clinical scale
stories.summary: At HIMSS 2026, Google Cloud showcased named enterprise healthcare deployments: Humana deployed Agent Assist for member service centers, CVS Health launched a health subsidiary with agentic AI, and Waystar used Gemini agents to prevent $15B+ in denied insurance claims — with agents processing patient records, voice, and medical imaging simultaneously. The case studies establish the first multi-organization evidence base for AI agents handling consequential healthcare workflows at production scale.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to 1960s hospital information systems adoption
stories.lateral_explanation: The first hospital information systems in the 1960s were rejected by physicians as bureaucratic overhead — until administrators demonstrated that automated billing and records retrieval saved enough clerical time to justify the disruption. Google Cloud's HIMSS 2026 showcase is the equivalent inflection point for AI agents: Waystar's $15B in prevented claim denials is the specific ROI case that converts skeptics, not because it's intellectually compelling but because it's operationally undeniable. Every technology wave in healthcare has needed exactly this proof case before scaling.

stories.headline: Alibaba launches Wukong: enterprise multi-agent platform for document editing, meetings, and approvals
stories.summary: Alibaba released Wukong — an enterprise AI agent platform managing multiple coordinated agents for document editing, meeting transcription, research synthesis, and approvals — with integrations planned for Slack, Teams, and WeChat. The platform is Alibaba's answer to Microsoft Copilot and Salesforce Agentforce: a full-stack enterprise agent coordinator built around Chinese enterprise infrastructure and designed for global expansion.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Chinese telegraph expansion 1870s
stories.lateral_explanation: China's first commercial telegraph lines in the 1870s were designed not just as communication tools but as coordination infrastructure for enterprise — allowing distant commercial offices to synchronize decisions without courier delay. Alibaba Wukong is structurally the same: a coordination layer that lets enterprise knowledge workers and AI agents synchronize across documents, meetings, research, and approvals without context-switching between disconnected systems. Both solved the same problem — how do you get coordinated action across a large distributed organization without losing information in transit?

stories.headline: Domo launches AI Agent Builder + MCP Server: connect governed enterprise data to any external AI tool
stories.summary: Domo released AI Agent Builder and MCP Server on March 25, enabling enterprises to create conversational agents and agentic workflows with direct access to governed enterprise data — and expose those agents to any external AI tool that supports MCP. The launch makes Domo's governed data layer interoperable with the entire MCP ecosystem, allowing agents built in Claude, GPT, or any other platform to query Domo data with the same access controls as internal systems.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to UNIX pipe philosophy
stories.lateral_explanation: The UNIX philosophy, articulated by Doug McIlroy in 1978, held that programs should do one thing well and connect to others through standard interfaces — the pipe operator making any two programs composable without redesigning either. Domo's AI Agent Builder with MCP Server applies the same principle to enterprise data: each agent does one thing (query governed data, answer a question, trigger a workflow), and MCP is the pipe that makes them composable without re-implementing the data access layer. A 1978 insight that shaped operating systems is now shaping enterprise AI architecture.

stories.headline: Palantir + NVIDIA deploy sovereign cloud AI agents: inference never leaves customer-controlled infrastructure
stories.summary: Palantir and NVIDIA announced integration of Nemotron 3 Super models running on Palantir's secure sovereign cloud infrastructure, enabling deployment of autonomous agents for government and enterprise systems where data and inference must remain in customer-controlled environments. The architecture makes sovereign AI — where neither the model weights nor the inference process ever crosses a vendor's infrastructure — commercially deployable for the first time at enterprise scale.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Swiss banking secrecy architecture
stories.lateral_explanation: Swiss banking secrecy wasn't just a legal construct — it was a physical architecture of air-gapped ledgers, separate customer record systems, and vault geography designed so sensitive data could never be compelled to leave Swiss jurisdiction. Palantir's sovereign cloud for AI agents is structurally identical: a technical architecture where neither data nor inference ever leaves the customer's controlled environment, making it legally and physically impossible for the AI vendor to access sensitive information. Sovereignty is now a compute feature, not just a policy declaration.

stories.headline: Anthropic Claude gains screen navigation and Dispatch: assign tasks from mobile and let Claude execute them
stories.summary: Anthropic expanded Claude's capabilities with screen navigation — enabling Claude to control user screens and complete multi-step tasks autonomously — and a Dispatch system allowing users to assign tasks to Claude directly from mobile devices. The Dispatch feature is the first asynchronous task delegation primitive from Anthropic: a user defines what they want done, dispatches it to Claude, and receives results without staying present in the conversation.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to military field radio dispatch protocols
stories.lateral_explanation: Military field radio protocols developed in WWII solved a specific problem: a commander needed to assign tasks to units in distant locations, confirm receipt, and track execution without physical presence. Anthropic's Dispatch feature is the civilian equivalent — a commander (user) assigns Claude to a specific task via mobile, with the same command-confirm-execute logic applied to knowledge work rather than battlefield operations. The management problem hasn't changed; the unit being commanded now runs on silicon and reports back asynchronously.

stories.headline: II Feria del Cómic de Madrid: 85 expositores y Juan Díaz Canales en Matadero del 26 al 29 de marzo
stories.summary: La segunda edición de la Feria del Cómic de Madrid reúne 85 casetas en las naves de Matadero con charlas de Juan Díaz Canales (Corto Maltés, Blacksad), Mia Oberländer y otros autores internacionales, junto a talleres sobre narrativa gráfica y cómic urbano madrileño y un mercado de originales. La feria continúa hoy y mañana, consolidándose como el evento central del cómic independiente en Madrid.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Gutenberg's movable type distribution network
stories.lateral_explanation: Gutenberg's 15th-century movable type didn't just make books cheaper — it created a distributed network of printers, distributors, and readers who could communicate ideas faster than any institution could suppress them. The comic book medium operates with the same structural logic: a distributed network of independent creators, small publishers, and bookshops that enables aesthetic and political experimentation outside mainstream cultural gatekeepers. The Feria del Cómic is not an entertainment event; it's a node in that network's annual reinforcement.

stories.headline: El Café Gijón reabre en marzo con nueva programación cultural de tertulias, conciertos y debates literarios
stories.summary: El Café Gijón, fundado en 1888 y escenario histórico de las tertulias literarias del siglo XX, reabre en marzo de 2026 con una programación cultural que incluye encuentros literarios, conciertos íntimos y debates sobre arte en el Paseo de Recoletos. El regreso recupera uno de los espacios más simbólicos de la vida intelectual madrileña, donde escritores como Gómez de la Serna, García Lorca y Cela desarrollaron ideas que definieron la literatura española del XX.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Viennese coffeehouse as intellectual commons
stories.lateral_explanation: The Viennese coffeehouse was not just a place to drink coffee but a paid intellectual workspace — writers, chess players, and revolutionaries rented time with a single cup to read newspapers, debate ideas, and incubate projects that couldn't happen in private apartments. The Café Gijón occupied the same function in 20th-century Madrid: a semi-public intellectual commons where ideas developed through conversation before becoming literature. The physical space of intellectual community has economic and social logic that digital platforms have consistently failed to replicate.

stories.headline: Ewa Juszkiewicz en el Thyssen: retratos flamencos del XVII con rostros sustituidos por flores y materia orgánica
stories.summary: La primera gran monográfica española de la pintora polaca Ewa Juszkiewicz reimagina retratos de mujeres de maestros flamencos y holandeses del siglo XVII sustituyendo sistemáticamente los rostros por flores, telas o elementos orgánicos que subvierten la mirada del pintor clásico. Visible hasta el 31 de mayo en el Thyssen-Bornemisza, la exposición propone una lectura crítica de 400 años de representación femenina en el canon europeo.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Byzantine iconoclasm controversy
stories.lateral_explanation: Byzantine iconoclasm in the 8th century was not about destroying beauty — it was about who had the authority to determine what sacred images looked like and who could be represented in them. Juszkiewicz's systematic replacement of female faces in canonical portraits operates with the same logic: it is not abstraction but deliberate deauthorization, making visible who controlled the image of women in 17th-century European painting. The canvas becomes the space where an old power relation is revealed by its own structured erasure.

stories.headline: An-Ki transforma el patio de Conde Duque en teatro de títeres inmersivo para 24 espectadores en Teatralia 2026
stories.summary: Dentro del festival Teatralia 2026, la Compañía Ortiga instala una carpa en el patio de Conde Duque para una experiencia de teatro de títeres y objetos para 24 espectadores simultáneos, explorando el origen de la humanidad con lenguaje visual y sensorial sin palabras. Las funciones continúan hasta el 29 de marzo, recuperando la tradición teatral que sitúa al espectador dentro de la acción en lugar de frente a ella.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval mystery play staging
stories.lateral_explanation: Medieval mystery plays were performed not on stages facing audiences but across multiple simultaneous locations in a town square — the audience moved between scenes, physically entering the narrative rather than watching it from a fixed point. An-Ki's 24-person immersive format is a direct structural return to this pre-proscenium theatrical logic, where the boundary between performer and witness is eliminated by spatial proximity and intimate scale. Theater that forces physical intimacy has always been about more than entertainment — it removes the psychological distance that allows audiences to remain passive observers.

stories.headline: La Casa Encendida cierra la primera retrospectiva española de Chiara Fumai con ciclo de cine feminista experimental
stories.summary: La primera retrospectiva en España de la artista italiana Chiara Fumai (1978–2017) concluye su ciclo de cine experimental en La Casa Encendida, con proyecciones que exploran espiritualidad, anarquismo y cuerpo femenino como lenguaje político. La obra de Fumai — fallecida a los 39 años — mezcla performance, escritura automática y archivos históricos del feminismo oculto para construir un universo que desafía las narrativas oficiales de la historia del arte.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to surrealist automatic writing as anti-rational act
stories.lateral_explanation: The Surrealists developed automatic writing in the 1920s not as an artistic technique but as a philosophical attack on rational consciousness — the idea that bypassing the editing mind could access truths that deliberate thought suppressed. Chiara Fumai's work inherits this structure but redirects it entirely: her automatic processes excavate specifically the truths that patriarchal historical narrative suppressed, using the irrational as a forensic tool rather than a purely aesthetic one. The method is the same; the archive being excavated has changed completely.

stories.headline: Último día: 'Alegorías de un Porvenir' en el Banco de España — 150 obras sobre arte y arquitectura institucional
stories.summary: Hoy es el último día para ver en el Banco de España la exposición 'Alegorías de un Porvenir', con 150 obras que incluyen planos originales de vidrieras, dibujos de archivo y fotografías históricas que exploran cómo los edificios del Banco de España fueron concebidos simultáneamente como estructuras financieras y declaraciones estéticas. La muestra documenta la intersección entre creación artística, arquitectura institucional y poder económico en la historia visual de Madrid.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Renaissance banking palace architecture
stories.lateral_explanation: The Medici and Fugger banking families of the 15th and 16th centuries built their headquarters as deliberate aesthetic statements — commissioning Brunelleschi and other master architects to signal stability, permanence, and taste in environments where trust was the primary commodity being sold. The Banco de España's architecture follows this exact tradition: buildings designed to make the abstract concept of institutional credibility legible through stone, proportion, and ornament. The exhibition reveals that institutional architecture has always been a form of financial communication, encoded in aesthetic choices rather than interest rates.

stories.headline: OpenAI Academy launches with AI Foundations certifications linked to Walmart, BCG, John Deere, and U.S. government hiring
stories.summary: OpenAI launched OpenAI Academy as a free AI education platform and is piloting its AI Foundations certification with employer partners including Walmart, John Deere, Lowe's, Boston Consulting Group, and major U.S. government agencies — making AI credentials directly connected to employer hiring pipelines for the first time. The partnership model bypasses traditional university credentialing, embedding OpenAI's certification into the hiring criteria of Fortune 500 companies and federal agencies.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to IBM PC certification ecosystem 1980s
stories.lateral_explanation: IBM's 1980s decision to publish its PC architecture specifications created an ecosystem of third-party certification programs that defined what 'computer literacy' meant for an entire generation of workers — the credential became the hiring signal even as the technology evolved. OpenAI Academy is attempting the same move: by getting Walmart, BCG, and government agencies to accept its AI Foundations credential, OpenAI is positioning itself as the authority on what AI competence means, not universities or professional associations. Whoever defines the credential defines the skill category itself.

stories.headline: AWS AI & ML Scholars 2026 Challenge Phase opens: certified AI Practitioner track via AWS Skill Builder
stories.summary: Amazon Web Services opened the Challenge phase of its 2026 AI & ML Scholars Program (March 24–June 24), offering structured learning through AWS Skill Builder toward the AWS Certified AI Practitioner credential, with cloud credits and continued access to labs for program graduates. The program specifically targets the emerging role of AI practitioners who need infrastructure-level understanding of how AI systems are deployed, not just how to use them.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Bell Labs internal technical education model
stories.lateral_explanation: Bell Laboratories in the 1950s built the most productive technical research environment in history partly by designing formal internal education programs that kept engineers continuously current with developments happening in other departments — the company invested in training because technical advantage compounds when skills are shared and updated across the organization. AWS's Scholars program follows the same principle applied to cloud AI infrastructure: organizations that train their people on how AI systems are actually built and deployed will have structural advantages over those that treat AI as a procurement decision rather than a technical competency.

stories.headline: Weill Cornell Medicine launches AIM program: AI agents predict disease progression for cancer and cardiovascular patients
stories.summary: Weill Cornell Medicine's Advancing Intelligence in Medicine (AIM) program integrates AI agents into clinical care by deploying precision medicine tools that predict disease progression and personalize treatment for cancer and cardiovascular conditions, with patient outcomes as the primary evaluation metric. The program marks a shift from AI as a diagnostic assistant to AI as a clinical decision architecture embedded in care workflows at one of the leading U.S. medical schools.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Bayesian reasoning in Victorian medicine
stories.lateral_explanation: Thomas Bayes' probability framework was applied to medicine as early as the 19th century — the idea that prior population data should update individual diagnosis probabilities — but clinical medicine resisted it for over 150 years because it conflicted with the intuition-based 'clinical art' tradition. Weill Cornell's AIM program is the institutional acceptance of Bayesian medicine at clinical scale: AI systems that update individual patient predictions from population data are doing exactly what Bayes described, now with enough data and compute to outperform clinical intuition in specific disease trajectories. The philosophical battle is over; the implementation is just beginning.

stories.headline: Purdue expands GenAI Studio and launches SciAgents: AI-assisted research infrastructure for thousands of students
stories.summary: Purdue University expanded its GenAI Studio infrastructure in 2026 by broadening the model catalog, deploying advanced agentic AI, and launching SciAgents — a program where AI agents assist faculty and graduate students in running scientific discovery workflows at scale. The expansion represents one of the largest institutional commitments to AI-integrated research infrastructure at a U.S. research university, moving AI from individual faculty experiments to standard research computing infrastructure.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to MIT Whirlwind computer institutional embedding
stories.lateral_explanation: MIT's Whirlwind computer in the early 1950s was the first research computer designed for real-time interaction, and its institutional embedding into MIT's culture created the template for university computing centers that shaped how every subsequent generation of researchers learned to think with machines. Purdue's SciAgents program is the 2026 version of this institutional embedding: building the infrastructure, culture, and research workflows around AI agents before individual faculty need to seek them out independently. The institutions that embedded computing earliest shaped computing culture; the same pattern is now repeating with agentic AI.

stories.headline: Sakana AI Scientist v2: AI-generated ML paper passes peer review in Nature — academia confronts non-human authorship
stories.summary: Sakana AI's AI Scientist v2 autonomously executed the full machine learning research lifecycle and produced the first fully AI-generated paper to pass rigorous human peer review, now published in Nature. The publication forces academia to confront a structural question its peer review system was not designed to answer: what does authorship, intellectual credit, and scientific accountability mean when the generator of the idea is a non-human system?
stories.lateral_hook: connects to ghost-writing in academic publishing history
stories.lateral_explanation: Ghost-writing in academic publishing has a long history — pharmaceutical companies paid medical writers to produce papers attributed to physicians in the 1990s, which passed peer review while hiding authorship — establishing the precedent that scientific content can be valid even when the attributed author didn't write it. Sakana's AI Scientist crosses into this territory with a critical difference: the AI is the actual intellectual source, not a ghost behind a human name. The peer review process designed to validate ideas regardless of source has now validated ideas from a non-human source, which means the system is working exactly as designed — and the implications haven't been processed yet.

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick's Wharton research on practical human-AI collaboration directly mirrors the enterprise patterns David has been tracking — especially the LangChain finding that human-in-the-loop beats full autonomy. This is the most evidence-backed book on what working alongside AI actually feels like day-to-day.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: With AI agent infrastructure maturing fast and MCP becoming standard protocol, Huyen's systems-level framework for production AI engineering is the most practical guide to the full stack — from evals to deployment patterns to agent orchestration. O'Reilly's most-read AI engineering book in 2026.
